NKJV.1: Therefore, holy brethren, partakers of the heavenly calling, consider the Apostle and High Priest of our confession, Christ Jesus,
NKJV.2: who was faithful to Him who appointed Him, as Moses also was faithful in all His house.
NKJV.3: For this One has been counted worthy of more glory than Moses, inasmuch as He who built the house has more honor than the house.
NKJV.4: For every house is built by someone, but He who built all things is God.
NKJV.5: And Moses indeed was faithful in all His house as a servant, for a testimony of those things which would be spoken afterward,
NKJV.6: but Christ as a Son over His own house, whose house we are if we hold fast the confidence and the rejoicing of the hope firm to the end.
NKJV.7: Therefore, as the Holy Spirit says:  “Today, if you will hear His voice,
NKJV.8: Do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion, In the day of trial in the wilderness,
NKJV.9: Where your fathers tested Me, tried Me, And saw My works forty years.
NKJV.10: Therefore I was angry with that generation, And said, ‘They always go astray in their heart, And they have not known My ways.’
NKJV.11: So I swore in My wrath, ‘They shall not enter My rest.’ ”
NKJV.12: Beware, brethren, lest there be in any of you an evil heart of unbelief in departing from the living God;
NKJV.13: but exhort one another daily, while it is called “Today,” lest any of you be hardened through the deceitfulness of sin.
NKJV.14: For we have become partakers of Christ if we hold the beginning of our confidence steadfast to the end,
NKJV.15: while it is said:  “Today, if you will hear His voice, Do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion.”
NKJV.16: For who, having heard, rebelled? Indeed, was it not all who came out of Egypt, led by Moses?
NKJV.17: Now with whom was He angry forty years? Was it not with those who sinned, whose corpses fell in the wilderness?
NKJV.18: And to whom did He swear that they would not enter His rest, but to those who did not obey?
NKJV.19: So we see that they could not enter in because of unbelief.

ESV.1: Therefore, holy brothers,  you who share in a heavenly calling, consider Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession,
ESV.2: who was faithful to him who appointed him, just as Moses also was faithful in all God's  house.
ESV.3: For Jesus has been counted worthy of more glory than Moses — as much more glory as the builder of a house has more honor than the house itself.
ESV.4: (For every house is built by someone, but the builder of all things is God.)
ESV.5: Now Moses was faithful in all God's house as a servant, to testify to the things that were to be spoken later,
ESV.6: but Christ is faithful over God's house as a son. And we are his house, if indeed we hold fast our confidence and our boasting in our hope.
ESV.7: Therefore, as the Holy Spirit says, “Today, if you hear his voice,
ESV.8: do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion, on the day of testing in the wilderness,
ESV.9: where your fathers put me to the test and saw my works for forty years.
ESV.10: Therefore I was provoked with that generation, and said, ‘They always go astray in their heart; they have not known my ways.’
ESV.11: As I swore in my wrath, ‘They shall not enter my rest.’”
ESV.12: Take care, brothers, lest there be in any of you an evil, unbelieving heart, leading you to fall away from the living God.
ESV.13: But exhort one another every day, as long as it is called “today,” that none of you may be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin.
ESV.14: For we have come to share in Christ, if indeed we hold our original confidence firm to the end.
ESV.15: As it is said, “Today, if you hear his voice, do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion.”
ESV.16: For who were those who heard and yet rebelled? Was it not all those who left Egypt led by Moses?
ESV.17: And with whom was he provoked for forty years? Was it not with those who sinned, whose bodies fell in the wilderness?
ESV.18: And to whom did he swear that they would not enter his rest, but to those who were disobedient?
ESV.19: So we see that they were unable to enter because of unbelief.

NASB.1: Therefore, holy brethren, partakers of a heavenly calling, consider Jesus, the Apostle and High Priest of our confession;
NASB.2: He was faithful to Him who appointed Him, as Moses also was in all His house.
NASB.3: For He has been counted worthy of more glory than Moses, by just so much as the builder of the house has more honor than the house.
NASB.4: For every house is built by someone, but the builder of all things is God.
NASB.5: Now Moses was faithful in all His house as a servant, for a testimony of those things which were to be spoken later;
NASB.6: but Christ [was faithful] as a Son over His house-- whose house we are, if we hold fast our confidence and the boast of our hope firm until the end.
NASB.7: Therefore, just as the Holy Spirit says, 'TODAY IF YOU HEAR HIS VOICE,
NASB.8: DO NOT HARDEN YOUR HEARTS AS WHEN THEY PROVOKED ME, AS IN THE DAY OF TRIAL IN THE WILDERNESS,
NASB.9: WHERE YOUR FATHERS TRIED [Me] BY TESTING [Me], AND SAW MY WORKS FOR FORTY YEARS.
NASB.10: 'THEREFORE I WAS ANGRY WITH THIS GENERATION, AND SAID, 'THEY ALWAYS GO ASTRAY IN THEIR HEART, AND THEY DID NOT KNOW MY WAYS';
NASB.11: AS I SWORE IN MY WRATH, 'THEY SHALL NOT ENTER MY REST.''
NASB.12: Take care, brethren, that there not be in any one of you an evil, unbelieving heart that falls away from the living God.
NASB.13: But encourage one another day after day, as long as it is [still] called 'Today,' so that none of you will be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin.
NASB.14: For we have become partakers of Christ, if we hold fast the beginning of our assurance firm until the end,
NASB.15: while it is said, 'TODAY IF YOU HEAR HIS VOICE, DO NOT HARDEN YOUR HEARTS, AS WHEN THEY PROVOKED ME.'
NASB.16: For who provoked [Him] when they had heard? Indeed, did not all those who came out of Egypt [led] by Moses?
NASB.17: And with whom was He angry for forty years? Was it not with those who sinned, whose bodies fell in the wilderness?
NASB.18: And to whom did He swear that they would not enter His rest, but to those who were disobedient?
NASB.19: [So] we see that they were not able to enter because of unbelief.

NLT.1: And so, dear brothers and sisters who belong to God and are partners with those called to heaven, think carefully about this Jesus whom we declare to be God’s messenger and High Priest.
NLT.2: For he was faithful to God, who appointed him, just as Moses served faithfully when he was entrusted with God’s entire house.
NLT.3: But Jesus deserves far more glory than Moses, just as a person who builds a house deserves more praise than the house itself.
NLT.4: For every house has a builder, but the one who built everything is God.
NLT.5: Moses was certainly faithful in God’s house as a servant. His work was an illustration of the truths God would reveal later.
NLT.6: But Christ, as the Son, is in charge of God’s entire house. And we are God’s house, if we keep our courage and remain confident in our hope in Christ.
NLT.7: That is why the Holy Spirit says, “Today when you hear his voice,
NLT.8:
don’t harden your hearts
as Israel did when they rebelled,
when they tested me in the wilderness.
NLT.9:
There your ancestors tested and tried my patience,
even though they saw my miracles for forty years.
NLT.10:
So I was angry with them, and I said,
‘Their hearts always turn away from me.
They refuse to do what I tell them.’
NLT.11:
So in my anger I took an oath:
‘They will never enter my place of rest.’”
NLT.12: Be careful then, dear brothers and sisters. Make sure that your own hearts are not evil and unbelieving, turning you away from the living God.
NLT.13: You must warn each other every day, while it is still “today,” so that none of you will be deceived by sin and hardened against God.
NLT.14: For if we are faithful to the end, trusting God just as firmly as when we first believed, we will share in all that belongs to Christ.
NLT.15:
Remember what it says: “Today when you hear his voice,
don’t harden your hearts
as Israel did when they rebelled.”
NLT.16: And who was it who rebelled against God, even though they heard his voice? Wasn’t it the people Moses led out of Egypt?
NLT.17: And who made God angry for forty years? Wasn’t it the people who sinned, whose corpses lay in the wilderness?
NLT.18: And to whom was God speaking when he took an oath that they would never enter his rest? Wasn’t it the people who disobeyed him?
NLT.19: So we see that because of their unbelief they were not able to enter his rest.

WEB.1: Therefore, holy brothers, partakers of a heavenly calling, consider the Apostle and High Priest of our confession, Jesus;
WEB.2: who was faithful to him who appointed him, as also was Moses in all his house.
WEB.3: For he has been counted worthy of more glory than Moses, because he who built the house has more honor than the house.
WEB.4: For every house is built by someone; but he who built all things is God.
WEB.5: Moses indeed was faithful in all his house as a servant, for a testimony of those things which were afterward to be spoken,
WEB.6: but Christ is faithful as a Son over his house; whose house we are, if we hold fast our confidence and the glorying of our hope firm to the end.
WEB.7: Therefore, even as the Holy Spirit says, “Today if you will hear his voice,
WEB.8: don’t harden your hearts, as in the rebellion, like as in the day of the trial in the wilderness,
WEB.9: where your fathers tested me by proving me, and saw my deeds for forty years.
WEB.10: Therefore I was displeased with that generation, and said, ‘They always err in their heart, but they didn’t know my ways;’
WEB.11: as I swore in my wrath, ‘They will not enter into my rest.’”
WEB.12: Beware, brothers, lest perhaps there be in any one of you an evil heart of unbelief, in falling away from the living God;
WEB.13: but exhort one another day by day, so long as it is called “today”; lest any one of you be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin.
WEB.14: For we have become partakers of Christ, if we hold fast the beginning of our confidence firm to the end:
WEB.15: while it is said, “Today if you will hear his voice, don’t harden your hearts, as in the rebellion.”
WEB.16: For who, when they heard, rebelled? No, didn’t all those who came out of Egypt by Moses?
WEB.17: With whom was he displeased forty years? Wasn’t it with those who sinned, whose bodies fell in the wilderness?
WEB.18: To whom did he swear that they wouldn’t enter into his rest, but to those who were disobedient?
WEB.19: We see that they were not able to enter in because of unbelief.

CSB17.1: Therefore, holy brothers and sisters, who share in a heavenly calling, consider Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.2: He was faithful to the one who appointed him, just as Moses was in all God’s household.
CSB17.3: For Jesus is considered worthy of more glory than Moses, just as the builder has more honor than the house.
CSB17.4: Now every house is built by someone, but the one who built everything is God. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.5: Moses was faithful as a servant <sup>ⓚ</sup> in all God’s household, as a testimony to what would be said in the future.
CSB17.6: But Christ was faithful as a Son over his household. And we are that household if we hold on to our confidence and the hope in which we boast. <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.7: Therefore, as the Holy Spirit says: Today, if you hear his voice,
CSB17.8:
do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion,
on the day of testing in the wilderness,
CSB17.9:
where your fathers tested me, tried me,
and saw my works
CSB17.10:
for forty years.
Therefore I was provoked to anger with that generation
and said, “They always go astray in their hearts,
and they have not known my ways.”
CSB17.11:
So I swore in my anger,
“They will not enter my rest.” [2] ⓜ
CSB17.12: Watch out, brothers and sisters, so that there won’t be in any of you an evil, unbelieving heart that turns away <sup>ⓝ</sup> from the living God.
CSB17.13: But encourage each other daily, ⓐ while it is still called today, so that none of you is hardened ⓑ by sin’s deception. ⓒ
CSB17.14: For we have become participants in Christ if we hold firmly until the end the reality <sup>[3]</sup> that we had at the start. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.15:
As it is said: Today, if you hear his voice,
do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion. [4] ⓔ
CSB17.16: For who heard and rebelled? Wasn’t it all who came out of Egypt under Moses? <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.17: With whom was God angry for forty years? Wasn’t it with those who sinned, whose bodies fell in the wilderness? <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.18: And to whom did he swear that they would not enter his rest, if not to those who disobeyed?
CSB17.19: So we see that they were unable to enter because of unbelief.

AMP.1: Therefore, holy brothers and sisters, who share in the heavenly calling, [thoughtfully and attentively] consider the Apostle and High Priest whom we confessed [as ours when we accepted Him as Savior], namely, Jesus;
AMP.2: He was faithful to Him who appointed Him [Apostle and High Priest], as Moses also was faithful in all God's house.
AMP.3: Yet Jesus has been considered worthy of much greater glory and honor than Moses, just as the builder of a house has more honor than the house.
AMP.4: For every house is built by someone, but the builder of all things is God.
AMP.5: Now Moses was faithful in [the administration of] all God's house, [but only] as a ministering servant, [his ministry serving] as a testimony of the things which were to be spoken afterward [the revelation to come in Christ];
AMP.6: but Christ is faithful as a Son over His [Father's] house. And we are His house if we hold fast our confidence and sense of triumph in our hope [in Christ].
AMP.7:
Therefore, just as the Holy Spirit says,
"Today if you hear His voice,
AMP.8:
Do not harden your hearts as [your fathers did] in the rebellion [of Israel at Meribah],
On the day of testing in the wilderness,
AMP.9:
Where your fathers tried Me by testing [My forbearance and tolerance],
And saw My works for forty years
[And found I stood their test].
AMP.10:
"Therefore I was angered with this generation,
And I said, 'They always go astray in their heart,
And they did not know My ways [nor become progressively better and more intimately acquainted with them]';
AMP.11:
So I swore [an oath] in My wrath,
'They shall not enter My rest [the promised land].'"
AMP.12: Take care, brothers and sisters, that there not be in any one of you a wicked, unbelieving heart [which refuses to trust and rely on the Lord, a heart] that turns away from the living God.
AMP.13: But continually encourage one another every day, as long as it is called "Today" [and there is an opportunity], so that none of you will be hardened [into settled rebellion] by the deceitfulness of sin [its cleverness, delusive glamour, and sophistication].
AMP.14: For we [believers] have become partakers of Christ [sharing in all that the Messiah has for us], if only we hold firm our newborn confidence [which originally led us to Him] until the end,
AMP.15:
while it is said,
"Today [while there is still opportunity] if you hear His voice,
Do not harden your heart, as when they provoked Me [in the rebellion in the desert at Meribah]."
AMP.16: For who were they who heard and yet provoked Him [with rebellious acts]? Was it not all those who came out of Egypt led by Moses?
AMP.17: And with whom was He angry for forty years? Was it not with those who sinned, whose dead bodies were scattered in the desert?
AMP.18: And to whom did He swear [an oath] that they would not enter His rest, but to those who disobeyed [those who would not listen to His word]?
AMP.19: So we see that they were not able to enter [into His rest—the promised land] because of unbelief and an unwillingness to trust in God.

MSG.1: So, my dear Christian friends, companions in following this call to the heights, take a good hard look at Jesus. He's the centerpiece of everything we believe,
MSG.2: faithful in everything God gave him to do. Moses was also faithful,
MSG.3: but Jesus gets far more honor. A builder is more valuable than a building any day.
MSG.4: Every house has a builder, but the Builder behind them all is God.
MSG.5: Moses did a good job in God's house, but it was all servant work, getting things ready for what was to come.
MSG.6: Christ as Son is in charge of the house. Now, if we can only keep a firm grip on this bold confidence, we're the house!
MSG.7: That's why the Holy Spirit says, Today, please listen;
MSG.8: don't turn a deaf ear as in "the bitter uprising," that time of wilderness testing!
MSG.9: Even though they watched me at work for forty years, your ancestors refused to let me do it my way; over and over they tried my patience.
MSG.10: And I was provoked, oh, so provoked! I said, "They'll never keep their minds on God; they refuse to walk down my road."
MSG.11: Exasperated, I vowed, "They'll never get where they're going, never be able to sit down and rest."
MSG.12: So watch your step, friends. Make sure there's no evil unbelief lying around that will trip you up and throw you off course, diverting you from the living God.
MSG.13: For as long as it's still God's Today, keep each other on your toes so sin doesn't slow down your reflexes.
MSG.14: If we can only keep our grip on the sure thing we started out with, we're in this with Christ for the long haul.
MSG.15: These words keep ringing in our ears: Today, please listen; don't turn a deaf ear as in the bitter uprising.
MSG.16: For who were the people who turned a deaf ear? Weren't they the very ones Moses led out of Egypt?
MSG.17: And who was God provoked with for forty years? Wasn't it those who turned a deaf ear and ended up corpses in the wilderness?
MSG.18: And when he swore that they'd never get where they were going, wasn't he talking to the ones who turned a deaf ear?
MSG.19: They never got there because they never listened, never believed.

NIV.1:
Jesus Greater Than Moses
Therefore, holy brothers, who share in the heavenly calling, fix your thoughts on Jesus, the apostle and high priest whom we confess.
NIV.2: He was faithful to the one who appointed him, just as Moses was faithful in all God’s house.
NIV.3: Jesus has been found worthy of greater honor than Moses, just as the builder of a house has greater honor than the house itself.
NIV.4: For every house is built by someone, but God is the builder of everything.
NIV.5: Moses was faithful as a servant in all God’s house, testifying to what would be said in the future.
NIV.6: But Christ is faithful as a son over God’s house. And we are his house, if we hold on to our courage and the hope of which we boast.
NIV.7:
Warning Against Unbelief
So, as the Holy Spirit says:
“Today, if you hear his voice,
NIV.8:
do not harden your hearts
as you did in the rebellion,
during the time of testing in the desert,
NIV.9:
where your fathers tested and tried me
and for forty years saw what I did.
NIV.10:
That is why I was angry with that generation,
and I said, ‘Their hearts are always going astray,
and they have not known my ways.’
NIV.11:
So I declared on oath in my anger,
‘They shall never enter my rest.’ ”
NIV.12: See to it, brothers, that none of you has a sinful, unbelieving heart that turns away from the living God.
NIV.13: But encourage one another daily, as long as it is called Today, so that none of you may be hardened by sin’s deceitfulness.
NIV.14: We have come to share in Christ if we hold firmly till the end the confidence we had at first.
NIV.15:
As has just been said:
“Today, if you hear his voice,
do not harden your hearts
as you did in the rebellion.”
NIV.16: Who were they who heard and rebelled? Were they not all those Moses led out of Egypt?
NIV.17: And with whom was he angry for forty years? Was it not with those who sinned, whose bodies fell in the desert?
NIV.18: And to whom did God swear that they would never enter his rest if not to those who disobeyed?
NIV.19: So we see that they were not able to enter, because of their unbelief.

NRSVCE.1: Therefore, brothers and sisters, holy partners in a heavenly calling, consider that Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession,
NRSVCE.2: was faithful to the one who appointed him, just as Moses also “was faithful in all God’s house.”
NRSVCE.3: Yet Jesus is worthy of more glory than Moses, just as the builder of a house has more honor than the house itself.
NRSVCE.4: (For every house is built by someone, but the builder of all things is God.)
NRSVCE.5: Now Moses was faithful in all God’s house as a servant, to testify to the things that would be spoken later.
NRSVCE.6: Christ, however, was faithful over God’s house as a son, and we are his house if we hold firm the confidence and the pride that belong to hope.
NRSVCE.7: Therefore, as the Holy Spirit says, “Today, if you hear his voice,
NRSVCE.8:
do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion,
as on the day of testing in the wilderness,
NRSVCE.9:
where your ancestors put me to the test,
though they had seen my works
NRSVCE.10:
for forty years.
Therefore I was angry with that generation,
and I said, ‘They always go astray in their hearts,
and they have not known my ways.’
NRSVCE.11:
As in my anger I swore,
‘They will not enter my rest.’”
NRSVCE.12: Take care, brothers and sisters, that none of you may have an evil, unbelieving heart that turns away from the living God.
NRSVCE.13: But exhort one another every day, as long as it is called “today,” so that none of you may be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin.
NRSVCE.14: For we have become partners of Christ, if only we hold our first confidence firm to the end.
NRSVCE.15:
As it is said, “Today, if you hear his voice,
do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion.”
NRSVCE.16: Now who were they who heard and yet were rebellious? Was it not all those who left Egypt under the leadership of Moses?
NRSVCE.17: But with whom was he angry forty years? Was it not those who sinned, whose bodies fell in the wilderness?
NRSVCE.18: And to whom did he swear that they would not enter his rest, if not to those who were disobedient?
NRSVCE.19: So we see that they were unable to enter because of unbelief.

NET.1: Therefore, holy brothers and sisters, partners in a heavenly calling, take note of Jesus, the apostle and high priest whom we confess,
NET.2: who is faithful to the one who appointed him, as Moses was also in God’s house.
NET.3: For he has come to deserve greater glory than Moses, just as the builder of a house deserves greater honor than the house itself!
NET.4: For every house is built by someone, but the builder of all things is God.
NET.5: Now Moses was faithful in all God’s house as a servant, to testify to the things that would be spoken.
NET.6: But Christ is faithful as a son over God’s house. We are of his house, if in fact we hold firmly to our confidence and the hope we take pride in.
NET.7: Therefore, as the Holy Spirit says, “Oh, that today you would listen as he speaks!
NET.8: “Do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion, in the day of testing in the wilderness.
NET.9: “There your fathers tested me and tried me, and they saw my works for forty years.
NET.10: “Therefore, I became provoked at that generation and said, ‘Their hearts are always wandering and they have not known my ways.’
NET.11: “As I swore in my anger, ‘They will never enter my rest!’”
NET.12: See to it, brothers and sisters, that none of you has an evil, unbelieving heart that forsakes the living God.
NET.13: But exhort one another each day, as long as it is called “Today,” that none of you may become hardened by sin’s deception.
NET.14: For we have become partners with Christ, if in fact we hold our initial confidence firm until the end.
NET.15: As it says, “Oh, that today you would listen as he speaks! Do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion.”
NET.16: For which ones heard and rebelled? Was it not all who came out of Egypt under Moses’ leadership?
NET.17: And against whom was God provoked for forty years? Was it not those who sinned, whose dead bodies fell in the wilderness?
NET.18: And to whom did he swear they would never enter into his rest, except those who were disobedient?
NET.19: So we see that they could not enter because of unbelief.
